subsection.name: Description
subsection.div: Checks that the whitespace around the square bracket tokens [ and ] are correct to the typical convention. The convention is not configurable.

ul.li: must not be preceded by whitespace when preceded by a type or identifier.
must not be followed by whitespace.
must not be preceded by whitespace.
must be followed by whitespace, except when it is followed by: another bracket, a dot, a comma, a semicolon, postfix operators (++, --), a right parenthesis, or a method reference (::).

pre.code:
class Example1 { int[] arr1; // ok, valid syntax int[][] arr2; // ok, valid syntax
void method(int[] p) { int i = p[0]; // ok, valid syntax int j = p [0]; // violation, '[' is preceded with whitespace int k = p[ 0]; // violation, '[' is followed by whitespace int x = p[0]++; // ok, ']' followed by postfix operator
int[] another = new int[5]; // ok, valid syntax
int a = p [1]; // violation, '[' preceded with whitespace int b = p[ 1]; // violation, '[' followed by whitespace int c = p[1 ]; // violation, ']' preceded with whitespace p[0]++; // ok, valid syntax p[0]+= 1; // violation, ']' not followed by whitespace } }

subsection.name: Violation Messages
subsection.p: All messages can be customized if the default message doesn't suit you. Please see the documentation to learn how to.

subsection.name: Fully Qualified Name
subsection.p: Use this fully qualified class name in configuration when an exact class reference is required.
